title: cochlearia draba
intro: Cochlearia draba, also known as the draba or whitlow grass, is a perennial plant native to the Arctic and subarctic regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Cochlearia draba can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems.
